title: Support passing of named volumes via Mounts API
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18408

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
API Support for passing volumes as mounts.
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@edsantiago I could not get the test to pass, although when I test by hand using 

curl --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock http://d/v4.5.0/libpod/containers/create -H "content-type: application/json" --data '{ "image": "alpine", "mounts":[{ "destination": "/test", "options": ["rw"], "source": "namevolume", "type": "volume"}], "name": "bar1" }'

It now works.

I am not strong on the APIV2.

@rhatdan hold your nose please because it's about to get ugly... sorry...

When I run into this (API does not return what I expected it to), my first line of attack is:
```diff
-t GET containers/$cid/json 200 \
+t GET containers/$cid/json 200 sdfsdf \
```
The `sdf` (or whatever) says "I want a 100% string comparison of results against sdf". Which of course will fail, but the failure message will dump the entire response:

I then use tmux's in-page search to look for `HostConfig`, but that's too long for my eyeballs to read, so I look for `Mounts` and see an empty array `[]`; I look for `scratch` and `volume` and see nothing. So... I guess I'm stuck there, because I don't know what I'm really looking for, but maybe this helps you?

Yes I saw the same thing, which left me a blank,  User was complaining about the following curl line.

```
$ curl --unix-socket /run/user/3267/podman/podman.sock http://d/v4.5.0/libpod/containers/create -H "content-type: application/json" --data '{ "image": "alpine", "mounts":[{ "destination": "/test", "options": ["rw"], "source": "dan", "type": "volume"}], "name": "bar1" }'
```
Which I fixed to get the mounts/volumes to be configured correctly.

The problem here is it seems that the test is attempting to set HostConfig.Mounts, which does not do the same thing as above from my perspective.

When I just use podman inspect to look at the data about the container in the first Curl example, I see Mounts, but it is not a child of HostConfig.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 465a1883f8c728140e0387c12268dba4b7cb5eb9. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
